title: carex tomentosa subsp. globifera
intro: Carex tomentosa subsp. globifera, also known as the globe-fruited tussock sedge, is a type of plant that belongs to the sedge family and is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carex tomentosa subsp. globifera include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce congestion, and provide relief from colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying and infusing the roots and leaves, and can be taken as a tea, tincture, or powder to reap its medicinal benefits.
